meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Project Management for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn advanced project management strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade software.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it is a failure of architectural visibility. By treating your codebase as a project management lifecycle, you isolate variables, document state transitions, and leverage AI agents like Claude Code to perform systematic root-cause analysis. Transform your chaotic debugging sessions into a structured, repeatable engineering process.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. You’ve been staring at a stack trace for three hours, and the logic feels like a tangled web of spaghetti code that defies all reason. You aren't just 'stuck'; you are experiencing the classic wall every developer hits when their mental model of the system no longer matches the reality of the machine.

But here is the secret: debugging is not a test of your memory; it is a test of your project management. When you stop chasing ghosts and start architecting your debugging workflow, you stop being a student who breaks things and start being an engineer who builds resilient, scalable systems.

steps.title: Define the Failure Boundary
steps.description: Before fixing, you must isolate the error. Document the exact state before the crash using a project management log.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and identify the specific module where the state transition fails. Create a structured debugging log template.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --analyze --path=./src/ --goal='identify state transition failure points'

steps.title: Architect the Reproduction Case
steps.description: A bug you cannot reproduce is a ghost. Build a minimal test script that triggers the failure reliably.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal reproduction script for the identified error in the auth module, focusing on edge cases.

steps.title: Trace Execution with AI Context
steps.description: Use Claude Code to trace the execution flow while you cross-reference with your project documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the execution flow of the current function. Identify where the data mutation deviates from the project specifications.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Debugging
steps.description: Apply patches that not only fix the bug but add logging for future observability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a fix for this logic error that includes robust error handling and telemetry hooks for future monitoring.

steps.title: Validate and Document Change
steps.description: Ensure the fix doesn't introduce regressions and update your project management backlog.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a post-mortem report for this bug and suggest preventative architectural changes.

pro_tips: Treat your IDE's 'Problems' tab as a project backlog: never ignore a warning, as they are often the precursors to silent bugs.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code: explain the logic line-by-line to the AI; the act of explanation often reveals the flaw.
Implement 'Feature Flags' for new code: if a bug appears, you can toggle the feature off without rolling back the entire build.
Keep a 'Bug Journal': tracking the time spent on specific error types helps you identify which parts of your architecture are high-risk.
Automate your regression testing: if a bug happens once, it is a mistake; if it happens twice, it is a process failure.
content.next_steps: Integrate these project management steps into your CI/CD pipeline and start tracking your 'Time-to-Resolve' for bugs.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to tackle any technical roadblock. Stop guessing and start engineering with a professional methodology that sets you apart from the crowd. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
